title: Reduce MCP tool response verbosity to cut token usage
description:
Three MCP tool handlers return excessively large responses, wasting token budget in every AI agent session; reducing their verbosity cuts overhead by 70–80% without information loss.

## Background

Analysis of 8 recent Claude Code sessions against the gf2 project measured tool-result character counts per MCP call. JIT tools account for 15.8% of all tool result content. Three tools dominate:

| Tool | Calls | Avg chars | Primary cost |
|------|-------|-----------|--------------|
| `jit_gate_check-all` | 4 | 6,760 | `stdout` from gate runs (87% of response) |
| `jit_issue_show` | 37 | 4,184 | `description` field (53–60%) + expanded `dependencies` (30–37%) |
| `jit_issue_update` | 30 | 4,016 | Returns full issue body — same bloat as show |

Root causes:

1. `GateRunResult.stdout` has no `#[serde(skip_serializing_if)]`. A passing `code-review` gate embeds the full AI review transcript (measured at 10,000 chars). The server already has a lean `GateRunSummary` in `crates/server/src/routes.rs:818` that omits stdout/stderr; the CLI does not use it.

2. `IssueShowResponse.description` is always serialized in full. For epics with 13 dependencies the expanded `Vec<MinimalIssue>` adds another 3,700–3,800 chars. `jit query all` already defaults to `MinimalIssue` and accepts `--full`; show/update do not.

3. `jit issue update` returns the complete updated `Issue` even though a mutation call needs only confirmation.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `jit gate check-all` omits `stdout` and `stderr` from passing gate runs by default; a `--verbose` flag (or equivalent) restores them. Failing gate runs retain stdout/stderr so the agent can diagnose the failure.
- [ ] `jit issue update` returns a lightweight confirmation response (`short_id`, `state`, `updated_at`, summary message) rather than the full issue body.
- [ ] `jit issue show` accepts a `--summary` flag that returns `MinimalIssue` + `gates_status` without the `description` field; the default behaviour is unchanged.
- [ ] All three changes are reflected in the MCP tool descriptions so agents know which flags to use.
- [ ] `cargo-ci` and `code-review` gates pass.

## Notes

Key source locations:
- `GateRunResult` struct: `crates/jit/src/domain/types.rs:584–619`
- `IssueShowResponse`: `crates/jit/src/output.rs:736–776`
- `gate check-all` CLI handler: `crates/jit/src/main.rs:1384–1432`
- `issue show` CLI handler: `crates/jit/src/main.rs:456–532`
- `issue update` CLI handler: `crates/jit/src/main.rs:535–656`
- `query all` lean/full pattern to replicate: `crates/jit/src/main.rs:2528–2571`
- Server `GateRunSummary` lean variant: `crates/server/src/routes.rs:818–852`
